researchers_stance: V6 is the strongest version in the study: 43% resonance, 32% intent, 2% dealbreakers (1/60). No copy change from V5; the lift reflects consistency of message with the ICP. Conversion confidence remains 0% — personas understand the offer and many would try it, but don't yet believe it will work for their real closet. This is the version to ship; iterate next on proof it works.
exec_summary: V6 repeated V5 copy and ICP-only audience (Victor, Rachel, Priya, Owen, Sam). Resonance rose 11pp to 43%, intent 12pp to 32%, dealbreakers fell to 2%. Conversion confidence stayed 0%. V6 is the final copy for submission.
findings:
• Winning version: V6 has the best resonance and intent with minimal dealbreakers.
  • What resonated: "You don't need more clothes. You need outfits that actually go together"; concrete examples (2pm meeting / 7pm drinks / 42°); visible $1.99; "What it looks like" sample output.
  • Conversion gap: 0% Agree+ conversion confidence in every round — next lever is proof it works.
  • Dealbreakers: Collapsed from 60% (V1) to 2% (V6) after ICP refocus and copy fixes.
recommendations:
1. Ship V6 for landing and early traffic.
  2. Improve conversion confidence next: add proof it works (e.g. before/after, week-1 results, dress-code handling).
  3. Don't retarget Dana/Sophia/Paul with this positioning; use this copy for Victor, Rachel, Priya, Owen, Sam.
qualitative_themes:
• Problem framing lands: "You don't need more clothes..." and "Does this look intentional, or just… random?" cited as strongest lines.
  • Startup-speak: "We'd rather earn trust than charge for potential" reads as performed humility to some; not a dealbreaker.
  • Trust in output: Personas want to see that it works for their closet/context before committing.
  • Price: $1.99 visible reduced suspicion; most rate it good_deal.
appendix_notes: V1–V4 used a broad 5-bucket audience (Paul, Dana, Sophia, Rachel, Victor). V5–V6 use ICP-only audience_v2 (Victor, Rachel, Priya, Owen, Sam). Cross-version comparison V5→V6 is directly comparable; V1–V4 include different segments.

bucket_deep_dives.Vibe Coder Victor:
Victor leads on intent (44%)

50% resonance, 44% intent, 0% dealbreakers. Strongest fit for the copy; problem framing and concrete examples land. Conversion confidence still 0% — skepticism about whether output will be better than self-pick.

Key personas: See results_v6.json for person_id and names in this bucket.

Risk: None; this is the core ICP.
bucket_deep_dives.Resident Rachel:
Rachel: 53% resonance, 33% intent

7% dealbreakers (1 of 12). Same winning lines; some want more proof it works for their context.

Risk: Low; one dealbreaker in segment.

Improved from V5; copy and positioning fit the recently-promoted, need-to-look-the-part segment.

Risk: Low.
